Electrical Engineering Class of 2026

From Simulation to Signal: RF Engineering That Works in the Real World

I'm Joel Kartchner. I am an RF and microwave engineer who moves comfortably from simulation to the lab bench. My work spans filter design and EM simulation at BYU, hands-on RF circuit testing at Raytheon, and building physical systems — from a 500kV Tesla coil to an 80GHz mmWave radar.

Invite Me to Interview View Writing & Projects

Interests: RF · microwave · radar · mmWave · filter design · S-parameters ·

Strengths Snapshot

Dual fluency in simulation (HFSS, ADS, SystemVue) and physical lab measurement (Network Analyzer, Spectrum Analyzer, Signal Generator)
Defense industry credibility — two summers at Raytheon working on RF products
Published IEEE conference author as an undergraduate
Breadth of RF sub-domains: filters, radar, photonics, laser fabrication, wireless comms

Highlights

  • Used HFSS to simulate RF thin film flex cables in a research setting.
  • Teaching assistant for Wireless Communication Circuits and EM Fields and Waves.
  • Tested and troubleshot RF circuit cards, wrote test code in Python, assisted with EME/EMI testing.

Selected Projects & Work

🎨

500,000 Volt Tesla Coil

Designed and built a 500,000 Volt Tesla Coil and safely demonstrated it to groups of people.

Designed and built a 500,000 Volt Tesla Coil and safely demonstrated it to groups of people.

🕹️

Laser Tag Game System and Laser Tag Grenade

Junior Team Design Project: Built a Laser Tag Game System and Laser Tag Grenade. Designed transmit and receive circuits, power systems and wrote game code and drivers.

Designed transmit and receive circuits, power systems and wrote game code and drivers.

🌊

mmWave Radar — 80GHz Radar System

Senior Capstone Project: Developed and built 80GHz radar system for detecting small high velocity projectiles in a laboratory setting.

Developed and built 80GHz radar system for detecting small high velocity projectiles in a laboratory setting.

Masters Research Assistant — BYU College of Electrical and Computer Engineering

Sept 2025–Present · Provo, UT

  • Used HFSS to simulate RF thin film flex cables in a research setting.
  • Designed and simulated Coupled Line and Stepped Impedance filters using Python and HFSS.

Teaching Assistant — BYU College of Electrical and Computer Engineering

Sept 2025–Present · Provo, UT

  • Teaching assistant for Wireless Communication Circuits and EM Fields and Waves.
  • Tutored students on topics related to Stub/Lumped Element Matching, Noise, Gain and Stability Circles.

RF Products Summer Intern — Raytheon

May 2024–August 2025 · Tucson, AZ

  • Tested and troubleshot RF circuit cards, wrote test code in Python, assisted with EME/EMI testing.
  • Used SystemVue and ADS to simulate RF exciters and receivers across frequency and temperature.
  • Tested circuits in a lab using Network and Spectrum Analyzers and Signal Generators.
  • Correlated simulated results to hardware measurements to validate circuit component changes.

Research Assistant — BYU College of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Laser Ablation Lab

May 2023–April 2025 · Provo, UT

  • Developed a laser ablation tool to create RF metal mesh filters and microscopic flexible cables.

Teaching Assistant — BYU College of Electrical and Computer Engineering

Sept 2023–Dec 2023 · Provo, UT

  • Tutored students on circuit analysis concepts and assisted students in creating circuits in the lab.

Research Assistant — BYU College of Electrical and Computer Engineering
